transcript.text: Thank you.
Hey Vivek, how are you?
Good, how are you?
Cannot complain, doing well.
Great to hear.
Should be a pretty short meeting.
Yeah.
Yeah, basically we have about 17 articles for you to review.
We're actually, we've been producing articles one week ahead, just given that the holidays-
I think Etisham is actually going to be done with pretty much all the articles, the topics that you had approved last week.
So you should have those coming in as well.
Okay, cool.
Yeah, so, and I'll be adding a few more just so that we start January, you know, hitting the ground running so that we don't have to start from scratch.
Sweet.
Yeah, so that'll come by your way.
Okay, just wanted to check, we do need the GA4, GSC access.
So we're still working on that.
I don't know what the holdup is, but I don't have access yet.
So I will, I'm going to make that happen.
Basically, that's a multi-time a week.
I bump my boss and say, hey, why, why can people not do this?
So IT mops, whoever's touching that is, in theory, that's happening this week.
But, uh, you know.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Is it still Evan who's working on that, or is it somebody else?
Yeah, Evan's still contracting mob stuff for us, but I don't know if because we switched to a new site and have a new domain, if there's like an IT holdup here or what.
But, yeah, our IT team is constantly swamped under a pile of asks, and I struggle to get high on that list.
Yeah, that makes sense.
But, you know, in theory, that is something that can be fixed.
And, like, I'm going to make clear this has to happen before the holidays.
Like, I can't – it can't turn into January, and I'm still asking about this.
So working on that.
On the articles, I started to look last night.
I got sidetracked, but I should be able to dedicate a few hours over the next couple of days here and just work through most of that.
So, yeah, anticipating getting through a lot of
That backlog, you know, today, tomorrow, and then the, yeah, the topics, I'll keep an eye out for those as they come in.
Topic creation has been going pretty smooth, so I'm sure that'll be great.
And yeah, I mean, obviously excited that everyone's pushing hard ahead of the holidays to just, you know, get ahead of that and not get held up by people being out and all that.
So, you know, definitely appreciative of the team's hard work on that.
Yeah, so I will actually be out the final two weeks of the year, so the week of Christmas and the week after that.
But there's going to be someone who will be checking the Slack channel, so if have any issues whatsoever, you know, just appreciative of the team.
Not that I think there should be anything.
One of the check, did Raj have any feedback at all?
Has he had the chance to look at some of the articles?
Yeah, so I need to go look at the doc and compare it to what we published.
So basically the cadence Will and I came up with is going to be like Raj can't be the, like this is not high enough value content on an individual piece basis for like Raj to be the bottleneck.
So I'm, as I'm going to publish things to our site, I am also linking to the original doc in like just a big running doc for Raj to look at.
He can at his own leisure go through and basically if he wants to go make any edits, I will then go push those edits again to the live site and I'll just find them and I'll make them.
Because, you know, Raj basically said, yeah, I can't, you know, obviously we decided this is important.
I'm not getting to it.
Like do what you guys do, what you got to do if you're okay being reactive to me.
So anyway, I need to go look at the first few because Raj told me that there were going to be a couple of small things, but I didn't see him come in.
And so anyway.
But if I find them, I will also, I will like, I will re-tag you guys on that and highlight it in the Slack channel.
Because if it's anything that we would want to do consistently, I'll highlight it.
If it's like a one-off wording thing, I'm not going to worry about, you know, the team trying to re-download that.
But in theory, sometime, you know, this week or next, we might get a couple of little mini-updates on terminology.

I also saw on Will's calendar, the real, the joys of Google Calendar, where your subordinates see things that you probably don't want to do, that they're doing some kind of session with, like, I think some kind of, like, messaging product marketer, like, freelancer or something, who was doing, like, some market research things on messaging and positioning.
I am very interested to see how that goes.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That did remind me of one thing that is a recent update on one of our video projects where Raj wants to – and I'll drop this off in writing as well.
Raj wants to lean into some terminology and positioning around data workflows as opposed to data pipelines when it's the context of a data analytics pipeline.

We kind of laid out the – we see data engineering pipelines as kind of core ETL and data ingestion and probably some of that core, if there's a giant data set that's going to get used a ton by every analyst, engineering might touch some of those pipelines.

Data analysts and data analytics pipelines are probably going to be more along the lines of queries off of cleaned up tables already or ingestion on more simple sources that I just need to do analysis off one source.
So ingesting –
There's not going to be any crazy matching going on and things like that.

It's interesting, off of my limited, like some interviews that I was doing with some people on positioning of some of our collateral analysts right now, a lot of them are like, yeah, no, I'd call that a data pipeline.
I'm not worried about that terminology.
And some of them are like, no, a data workflow does make more sense to me.

So anyway, we are exploring where those lines are and at different types of orgs and like, who in our audience would call this a data pipeline or a data analysis pipeline or a data analytics pipeline?
Who would call this like a data analytics workflow?

You know, the people who have spent all of their time in BI tools might be a little more likely to use the word workflow than the people who have some more like real SQL and like hands-on experience in a warehouse.
So anyway, I'm hoping for more clarity on that because Raj has had a...

Let's adjust on some video content to move away from like overemphasizing pipelines in some like product feature demos, more like looking at individual features of the product and doing little mini demo videos on them.
So Raj has had us do that.
That said, I don't think the word workflow is anywhere on the main like brochure site right now for prophecy.ai.
So I think they're trying to figure out what's going to win there.
No matter what, I'm going to have some go fix it work done between videos and written content because right now they don't match.
Sorry for you.
You know, but it's a good job security.
Someone's got to do it.
So, but, uh, so anyway, I will, I can drop off that like idea and maybe we, and maybe that'll be useful for Enisham where it's like, Hey, go like, go find 510, like data.
Yeah.
Workflow type, like, you know, opportunities for us that we know no matter what is, it's close enough that it's relevant for us for like AEO, GEO content.
And then, you know, stay tuned on if I do anything else with this, but.
Yeah.
Yeah, that makes sense.
That'll be super helpful when you get it over to us.
Yeah.
From our perspective, the mission essentially is like getting you the top line traffic, right?
As we discussed, like, that's the thing.
So I'm seeing there might be a bit of a clash there between, like, there might be a situation where, you know, data pipelines is a little more popular.
Yeah.
It's being searched more often versus workflow.
We'll try to massage that in.
There's ways, you know, we can do that.
But something else I want to point out is since Raj is kind of randomly reviewing topics in your sheet, I do want to point out that the articles, we've been kind of slowly evolving.
The first few articles that we sent were super focused on prophecies, POV, and zeroing in on that.

What we found is that, just in terms of best practices, as we move forward, what we found is it's a lot more effective to essentially address the topic as the algorithm expects it to be addressed, and then have maybe one or two dedicated sections where we really dive into what prophecies POV is.
So just want to surface that because he might read one of these articles and be like, who are we talking to?
Like, this is not our audience, but is this important to give that context?
So just want to highlight that.
Yeah, definitely.
I think that's very fair.
And yeah, I've noticed that when I'm reading.
I'm still happy of like, we're getting to some prophecy POV at the end.
We're giving someone some reason to think about us as a source beyond just the first couple of paragraphs, you
I think it's checking some of the like branded content boxes that matter for us.
So yeah, I definitely hear you on the strategy there.
That makes a lot of sense to me.
If I get any negative feedback or I open up a doc and it's like covered in comments, I will look out for that perspective.
I can, Raj is back in the States now as of like last Monday or something.
He was in India for like two months.
So I also have a lot more access to like, hey Raj, do you have 10 minutes than I did for the last couple of months?
So, you know, in general, I think that makes sense and he should be overall open to that.
And then, yeah, one more thought on the like the pipeline versus workflow and like where's the traffic at?
What are people searching?
You know, AEO, GE, SEO, SEO.
Definitely because we're publishing this content and guides, I definitely hear you on the we need to keep using data pipelines because there's absolutely going to be way more volume there.
And I think that like...
like...
If we get to the place where it's like, from an editorial perspective, I am worried about how prophecy is messaging on it.
I do think that's where those things where it's like, I am fine with, you know, data analytics pipelines, sometimes referred to as data analytics workflows or whatever.
Just throwing in clauses like that, that's like, you know, basically have some qualifiers in there.
Like, if we run into a scenario where I'm like, hey, from an editorial perspective, this doesn't quite sound like us.
But right now, we're going to keep, let's keep charging on whatever topics you guys have done the research on and how you think that we should be targeting, you know, those topics.
And I'm totally fine with that.
Let's still, well, let's throw in a few data workflow type ones anyway.
Who knows?
Maybe we land on one of those that kind of like low hanging fruit.
There's less competition.
We can go win on a like, you know, lower volume query anyway.
And that's nice.
But yeah, that makes sense.
Yeah.
Cool.
All right.
So I'll wait for your update regarding the messaging, and then we will, yeah, we'll just keep continuing as we are.
Sweet.
Will just literally slapped me right now and said, are there any metrics we can report on in the AEOG work with GrowthX, or is that blocked with GA4?
So I just had the opportunity to say that is still blocked.
Someone's asking him questions now that he can now go to IT or whatever and say, well, now Raj wants to know, so will you just do it?
So there we go.
So this will probably get unblocked like tomorrow morning now, so.
Yeah, that should be good.
Yeah, we still will only be able to report from when we get added.
So it's going to be, you know, it's not going to be retroactive, but yeah, it's good that it's going.
I want to ask you real quick as well.
Do you know Scott Stransky at all?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
Okay, cool.
We connected over, I don't know, like LinkedIn content marketer people kind of thing.
Oh my God.
And then, yeah, we had a call, a couple months ago, we hopped on a call together for a little bit, and we're just chatting marketing and some other things that I'm working on, kind of on the side.
And I think he mentioned that he was connected with you.
He's like, do you know Vivek?
And I'm like, oh, yeah, no, we've worked a ton with GrowthX.
okay, yeah, because Starter is effectively my neighbor over here.
Yeah, as I say, aren't you guys, like, down the road from each other or something?
Yeah, yeah, yeah.
He sees right here.
So, we were talking about prophecy, cetera.
This was when I was off the account, and then he was like, yeah, I know Cody, and I was like, oh, , okay.
Yeah, it is weird to me how small the content marketing world has kind of become on, like, LinkedIn.
Like, I'll every now and then see you comment on something.
I'm like, oh, Vivek knows them, too.
Look at that.
I don't know this guy very well or whatever.
But I see a lot of that, and it's crazy to me how many people are just, like, right in your little neck of the woods now.
Yeah.
Clearly the, like, cop...
Cost of living meets bang for the buck on like quality of living thing is working for people where you guys are at.
So, you know, I only ever hear great things.
Yeah, it has become pretty popular.
Like I think the content marketing hub, so to speak, for Europe has become London and Lisbon.
For the US, I think it's like Colorado for some weird reason.
Like everybody that knows in Colorado.
Yeah, there's a bunch of people there.
And like even like, do you know the like the screen space guys?
They do the like kind of like interactive product demo walkthrough things.
They're, you know, they do like the kind of a storytelling content marketing things there in Colorado.
Like even some of the like niche corners of it seem like they've landed there somehow.
I definitely feel like half the content marketers are connected.
I was there briefly.
I'm not nearly as active on LinkedIn as the like real content marketing community.
I just learned for the most part, but, you know.
Yeah, well, good to know.
Small world, yeah, yeah, man, cool, all right, all right, cool, I'll follow up on Slack, and yeah, we'll take it from there.
Sweet, awesome, thanks, Vivek, talk soon, you, bye, bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Sync on content production, analytics access, and messaging strategy.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Analytics Access Unblocked:** GA4/GSC access, critical for reporting, is now prioritized for completion before the holidays.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=910.0)
  - [**Content Production Ahead:** 17 articles are ready for review, with more in production to ensure a fast start in January.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
  - [**Messaging Shift:** Raj wants to use "data workflows" over "data pipelines" for analytics content, creating a content alignment task.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [**SEO Strategy:** The primary strategy remains targeting high-volume "data pipelines" keywords, with a secondary test of low-competition "data workflows" topics.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)

## Topics

### Analytics Access

  - [GA4 & GSC access is the primary blocker for reporting on AEO/SEO performance.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=910.0)
  - [**Cause:** Internal IT delays, possibly related to a new site/domain migration.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=241.0)
  - [**Resolution:** Cody has escalated the request, setting a pre-holiday deadline.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=910.0)
      - [**Catalyst:** Will's direct inquiry to Cody about metrics prompted an immediate escalation to IT.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=910.0)

### Content Production & Review

  - [**Production Cadence:** GrowthX is producing content one week ahead of schedule to maintain momentum through the holidays.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
      - [17 articles are ready for review.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
      - [More topics are in production for a fast start in January.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=183.0)
  - [**Review Process:** Raj's review is asynchronous to prevent it from becoming a bottleneck.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
      - [Cody publishes articles, then links to the source docs in a central doc for Raj's review.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=343.0)
      - [Cody will implement any edits Raj requests.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=360.0)
      - [Cody will flag systemic feedback in Slack for GrowthX's team.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=420.0)

### Messaging Strategy: "Pipelines" vs. "Workflows"

  - [**Directive:** Raj wants to use "data workflows" instead of "data pipelines" for analytics-focused content.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=480.0)
  - [**Rationale:** The terms may appeal to different user personas.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=546.0)
      - [**"Pipelines"** → Users with deep SQL/warehouse experience.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=580.0)
      - [**"Workflows"** → Users primarily in BI tools.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=580.0)
  - [**Challenge:** "Data pipelines" has significantly higher search volume, which conflicts with the SEO goal of driving top-line traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=683.0)
  - [**Resolution:**](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)
      - [**Primary Strategy:** Continue targeting high-volume "data pipelines" keywords.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=816.0)
      - [**Secondary Strategy:** Test a few "data workflow" topics to find low-competition opportunities.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
      - [**Editorial Tactic:** Use qualifying clauses (e.g., "data analytics pipelines, sometimes referred to as data analytics workflows") to bridge the terminology gap.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=840.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Cody:**](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
      - [Secure GA4/GSC access before the holidays.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
      - [Review the 17 pending articles.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=284.0)
      - [Provide GrowthX with 5–10 "data workflow" topic ideas.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=647.0)
  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)
      - [Incorporate "data workflow" topics into the content plan.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=882.0)
      - [Maintain the current production cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/ccRj8ddWN_JDRzFLZJFRq8ssJz1wVHPM?tab=summary&timestamp=168.0)

action_items.description: Add Jan topics to content plan; send to Cody
Review 17 articles; send feedback to Vivek
Compare published vs Raj doc; post Slack updates for Raj-requested changes
Post Slack guidance re: 'data workflows' vs 'pipelines' for Etisham; request 5–10 workflow topics
